target.name: cylib
target.founder: Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, Paul Sabarny

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Cylib is a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, and Paul Sabarny. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies—Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy—alongside a broader universe of 8 private climate-tech firms and 1 public comparable (LNZA).

The peer-median method applied to the three primary comparables yields a valuation of $150M. This reflects valuations ranging from $110M (Steady Energy) to $200M (Graphyte), with Fourth Power at $150M. The broader comparable universe includes later-stage and adjacent players such as TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials, which provides sector context but is less directly applicable at Series A stage. Given the limited sample size of exactly matched peers, the peer-median estimate carries material weight but should be treated as a point estimate rather than a definitive anchor.

The triangulated valuation range supports a low of $128M, median of $150M, and high of $173M. Cylib has raised €140M+ in capital, including a €55M Series A plus €26.1M in NRW grant funding and €63.4M in BMWE grant funding. Key uncertainties include the small peer sample (n=3), limited applicability of the single public comparable, and the absence of revenue or growth metrics in the provided data set. The range is appropriately wide given these constraints.
